Fields.Name: Collaborative
Fields.Description: `true` if the owner allows other users to modify the playlist.

Fields.Name: Description
Fields.Description: The playlist description. _Only returned for modified, verified playlists, otherwise_ `null`.

Fields.Name: ExternalUrls
Fields.Description: Known external URLs for this playlist.

Fields.Name: Followers
Fields.Description: Information about the followers of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Href
Fields.Description: A link to the Web API endpoint providing full details of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Id
Fields.Description: The [Spotify ID](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the playlist.

Fields.Name: Images
Fields.Description: Images for the playlist. The array may be empty or contain up to three images. The images are returned by size in descending order. See [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists). _**Note**: If returned, the source URL for the image (`url`) is temporary and will expire in less than a day._

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Owner
Fields.Description: The user who owns the playlist

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: The playlist's public/private status: `true` the playlist is public, `false` the playlist is private, `null` the playlist status is not relevant. For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

Fields.Name: SnapshotId
Fields.Description: The version identifier for the current playlist. Can be supplied in other requests to target a specific playlist version

Fields.Name: Tracks
Fields.Description: The tracks of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Type
Fields.Description: The object type: "playlist"

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: The [Spotify URI](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the playlist.

Fields.Description: The playlist description. _Only returned for modified, verified playlists, otherwise_ `null`.

Fields.Name: ExternalUrls
Fields.Description: Known external URLs for this playlist.

Fields.Name: Href
Fields.Description: A link to the Web API endpoint providing full details of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Id
Fields.Description: The [Spotify ID](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the playlist.

Fields.Name: Images
Fields.Description: Images for the playlist. The array may be empty or contain up to three images. The images are returned by size in descending order. See [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists). _**Note**: If returned, the source URL for the image (`url`) is temporary and will expire in less than a day._

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the playlist.

Fields.Name: Owner
Fields.Description: The user who owns the playlist

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: The playlist's public/private status: `true` the playlist is public, `false` the playlist is private, `null` the playlist status is not relevant. For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

Fields.Name: SnapshotId
Fields.Description: The version identifier for the current playlist. Can be supplied in other requests to target a specific playlist version

Fields.Name: Tracks
Fields.Description: A collection containing a link ( `href` ) to the Web API endpoint where full details of the playlist's tracks can be retrieved, along with the `total` number of tracks in the playlist. Note, a track object may be `null`. This can happen if a track is no longer available.

Fields.Name: Type
Fields.Description: The object type: "playlist"

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: The [Spotify URI](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the playlist.

Fields.Name: Album
Fields.Description: The album on which the track appears. The album object includes a link in `href` to full information about the album.

Fields.Name: Artists
Fields.Description: The artists who performed the track. Each artist object includes a link in `href` to more detailed information about the artist.

Fields.Name: AvailableMarkets
Fields.Description: A list of the countries in which the track can be played, identified by their [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) code.

Fields.Name: DiscNumber
Fields.Description: The disc number (usually `1` unless the album consists of more than one disc).

Fields.Name: DurationMs
Fields.Description: The track length in milliseconds.

Fields.Name: Explicit
Fields.Description: Whether or not the track has explicit lyrics ( `true` = yes it does; `false` = no it does not OR unknown).

Fields.Name: ExternalIds
Fields.Description: Known external IDs for the track.

Fields.Name: ExternalUrls
Fields.Description: Known external URLs for this track.

Fields.Name: Href
Fields.Description: A link to the Web API endpoint providing full details of the track.

Fields.Name: Id
Fields.Description: The [Spotify ID](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the track.

Fields.Name: IsPlayable
Fields.Description: Part of the response when [Track Relinking](/documentation/web-api/concepts/track-relinking) is applied. If `true`, the track is playable in the given market. Otherwise `false`.

Fields.Name: LinkedFrom
Fields.Description: Part of the response when [Track Relinking](/documentation/web-api/concepts/track-relinking) is applied, and the requested track has been replaced with different track. The track in the `linked_from` object contains information about the originally requested track.

Fields.Name: Restrictions
Fields.Description: Included in the response when a content restriction is applied.

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the track.

Fields.Name: Popularity
Fields.Description:
The popularity of the track. The value will be between 0 and 100, with 100 being the most popular.
The popularity of a track is a value between 0 and 100, with 100 being the most popular. The popularity is calculated by algorithm and is based, in the most part, on the total number of plays the track has had and how recent those plays are.
Generally speaking, songs that are being played a lot now will have a higher popularity than songs that were played a lot in the past. Duplicate tracks (e.g. the same track from a single and an album) are rated independently. Artist and album popularity is derived mathematically from track popularity. _**Note**: the popularity value may lag actual popularity by a few days: the value is not updated in real time._

Fields.Name: PreviewUrl
Fields.Description: A link to a 30 second preview (MP3 format) of the track. Can be `null`

Fields.Name: TrackNumber
Fields.Description: The number of the track. If an album has several discs, the track number is the number on the specified disc.

Fields.Name: Type
Fields.Description: The object type: "track".

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: The [Spotify URI](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the track.

Fields.Name: IsLocal
Fields.Description: Whether or not the track is from a local file.

Fields.Name: AudioPreviewUrl
Fields.Description: A URL to a 30 second preview (MP3 format) of the chapter. `null` if not available.

Fields.Name: AvailableMarkets
Fields.Description: A list of the countries in which the chapter can be played, identified by their [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) code.

Fields.Name: ChapterNumber
Fields.Description: The number of the chapter

Fields.Name: Description
Fields.Description: A description of the chapter. HTML tags are stripped away from this field, use `html_description` field in case HTML tags are needed.

Fields.Name: HtmlDescription
Fields.Description: A description of the chapter. This field may contain HTML tags.

Fields.Name: DurationMs
Fields.Description: The chapter length in milliseconds.

Fields.Name: Explicit
Fields.Description: Whether or not the chapter has explicit content (true = yes it does; false = no it does not OR unknown).

Fields.Name: ExternalUrls
Fields.Description: External URLs for this chapter.

Fields.Name: Href
Fields.Description: A link to the Web API endpoint providing full details of the chapter.

Fields.Name: Id
Fields.Description: The [Spotify ID](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the chapter.

Fields.Name: Images
Fields.Description: The cover art for the chapter in various sizes, widest first.

Fields.Name: IsPlayable
Fields.Description: True if the chapter is playable in the given market. Otherwise false.

Fields.Name: Languages
Fields.Description: A list of the languages used in the chapter, identified by their [ISO 639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639) code.

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the chapter.

Fields.Name: ReleaseDate
Fields.Description: The date the chapter was first released, for example `"1981-12-15"`. Depending on the precision, it might be shown as `"1981"` or `"1981-12"`.

Fields.Name: ReleaseDatePrecision
Fields.Description: The precision with which `release_date` value is known.

Fields.Name: ResumePoint
Fields.Description: The user's most recent position in the chapter. Set if the supplied access token is a user token and has the scope 'user-read-playback-position'.

Fields.Name: Type
Fields.Description:
The object type.

**Value**: `"episode"`

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: The [Spotify URI](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the chapter.

Fields.Name: Restrictions
Fields.Description: Included in the response when a content restriction is applied.

Fields.Name: AlbumType
Fields.Description: The type of the album.

Fields.Name: TotalTracks
Fields.Description: The number of tracks in the album.

Fields.Name: AvailableMarkets
Fields.Description: The markets in which the album is available: [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2). _**NOTE**: an album is considered available in a market when at least 1 of its tracks is available in that market._

Fields.Name: ExternalUrls
Fields.Description: Known external URLs for this album.

Fields.Name: Href
Fields.Description: A link to the Web API endpoint providing full details of the album.

Fields.Name: Id
Fields.Description: The [Spotify ID](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the album.

Fields.Name: Images
Fields.Description: The cover art for the album in various sizes, widest first.

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the album. In case of an album takedown, the value may be an empty string.

Fields.Name: ReleaseDate
Fields.Description: The date the album was first released.

Fields.Name: ReleaseDatePrecision
Fields.Description: The precision with which `release_date` value is known.

Fields.Name: Restrictions
Fields.Description: Included in the response when a content restriction is applied.

Fields.Name: Type
Fields.Description:
The object type.

**Value**: `"album"`

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: The [Spotify URI](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) for the album.

Fields.Name: Artists
Fields.Description: The artists of the album. Each artist object includes a link in `href` to more detailed information about the artist.

Fields.Name: Tracks
Fields.Description: The tracks of the album.

Fields.Name: Copyrights
Fields.Description: The copyright statements of the album.

Fields.Name: ExternalIds
Fields.Description: Known external IDs for the album.

Fields.Name: Genres
Fields.Description: A list of the genres the album is associated with. If not yet classified, the array is empty.

Fields.Name: Label
Fields.Description: The label associated with the album.

Fields.Name: Popularity
Fields.Description: The popularity of the album. The value will be between 0 and 100, with 100 being the most popular.

Nodes.Title: Me following Request 1

Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: ContextUri
Fields.Description:
Optional. Spotify URI of the context to play.
Valid contexts are albums, artists & playlists.
`{context_uri:"spotify:album:1Je1IMUlBXcx1Fz0WE7oPT"}`

Fields.Name: Uris
Fields.Description:
Optional. A JSON array of the Spotify track URIs to play.
For example: `{"uris": ["spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh", "spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M"]}`

Fields.Name: Offset
Fields.Description:
Optional. Indicates from where in the context playback should start. Only available when context_uri corresponds to an album or playlist object
"position" is zero based and can’t be negative. Example: `"offset": {"position": 5}`
"uri" is a string representing the uri of the item to start at. Example: `"offset": {"uri": "spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M"}`

Fields.Name: PositionMs
Fields.Description: Indicates from what position to start playback. Must be a positive number. Passing in a position that is greater than the length of the track will cause the player to start playing the next song.

Nodes.Title: Me Player Play Request
Nodes.Name: MePlayerPlayRequest
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: DeviceIds
Fields.Description:
A JSON array containing the ID of the device on which playback should be started/transferred.
For example:`{device_ids:["74ASZWbe4lXaubB36ztrGX"]}`
_**Note**: Although an array is accepted, only a single device_id is currently supported. Supplying more than one will return `400 Bad Request`_

Fields.Name: Play
Fields.Description:
**true**: ensure playback happens on new device.
**false** or not provided: keep the current playback state.

Nodes.Title: Paged Categories
Nodes.Name: PagedCategories
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: Defaults to `true`. If `true` the playlist will be included in user's public playlists, if `false` it will remain private.

Nodes.Title: Playlists Followers Request
Nodes.Name: PlaylistsFollowersRequest
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The new name for the playlist, for example `"My New Playlist Title"`

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: If `true` the playlist will be public, if `false` it will be private.

Fields.Name: Collaborative
Fields.Description:
If `true`, the playlist will become collaborative and other users will be able to modify the playlist in their Spotify client.
_**Note**: You can only set `collaborative` to `true` on non-public playlists._

Fields.Name: Description
Fields.Description: Value for playlist description as displayed in Spotify Clients and in the Web API.

Nodes.Title: Playlists Request
Nodes.Name: PlaylistsRequest
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: Uris
Fields.Description:
A JSON array of the [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) to add. For example: `{"uris": ["spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh","spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M", "spotify:episode:512ojhOuo1ktJprKbVcKyQ"]}`
A maximum of 100 items can be added in one request. _**Note**: if the `uris` parameter is present in the query string, any URIs listed here in the body will be ignored._

Fields.Name: Position
Fields.Description: The position to insert the items, a zero-based index. For example, to insert the items in the first position: `position=0` ; to insert the items in the third position: `position=2`. If omitted, the items will be appended to the playlist. Items are added in the order they appear in the uris array. For example: `{"uris": ["spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh","spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M"], "position": 3}`

Nodes.Title: Playlists Tracks Request
Nodes.Name: PlaylistsTracksRequest
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: RangeStart
Fields.Description: The position of the first item to be reordered.

Fields.Name: InsertBefore
Fields.Description:
The position where the items should be inserted.
To reorder the items to the end of the playlist, simply set _insert_before_ to the position after the last item.
Examples:
To reorder the first item to the last position in a playlist with 10 items, set _range_start_ to 0, and _insert_before_ to 10.
To reorder the last item in a playlist with 10 items to the start of the playlist, set _range_start_ to 9, and _insert_before_ to 0.

Fields.Name: RangeLength
Fields.Description:
The amount of items to be reordered. Defaults to 1 if not set.
The range of items to be reordered begins from the _range_start_ position, and includes the _range_length_ subsequent items.
Example:
To move the items at index 9-10 to the start of the playlist, _range_start_ is set to 9, and _range_length_ is set to 2.

Fields.Name: SnapshotId
Fields.Description: The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes.

Nodes.Title: Playlists Tracks Request 1
Nodes.Name: PlaylistsTracksRequest1
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: Tracks
Fields.Description:
An array of objects containing [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) of the tracks or episodes to remove.
For example: `{ "tracks": [{ "uri": "spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh" },{ "uri": "spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M" }] }`. A maximum of 100 objects can be sent at once.

Fields.Name: SnapshotId
Fields.Description:
The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes.
The API will validate that the specified items exist and in the specified positions and make the changes,
even if more recent changes have been made to the playlist.

Nodes.Title: Playlists Tracks Request 2
Nodes.Name: PlaylistsTracksRequest2
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Nodes.Title: Playlist Snapshot Id
Nodes.Name: PlaylistSnapshotId

Fields.Name: NumSamples
Fields.Description: The exact number of audio samples analyzed from this track. See also `analysis_sample_rate`.

Fields.Name: Duration
Fields.Description: Length of the track in seconds.

Fields.Name: SampleMd5
Fields.Description: This field will always contain the empty string.

Fields.Name: OffsetSeconds
Fields.Description: An offset to the start of the region of the track that was analyzed. (As the entire track is analyzed, this should always be 0.)

Fields.Name: WindowSeconds
Fields.Description: The length of the region of the track was analyzed, if a subset of the track was analyzed. (As the entire track is analyzed, this should always be 0.)

Fields.Name: AnalysisSampleRate
Fields.Description: The sample rate used to decode and analyze this track. May differ from the actual sample rate of this track available on Spotify.

Fields.Name: AnalysisChannels
Fields.Description: The number of channels used for analysis. If 1, all channels are summed together to mono before analysis.

Fields.Name: EndOfFadeIn
Fields.Description: The time, in seconds, at which the track's fade-in period ends. If the track has no fade-in, this will be 0.0.

Fields.Name: StartOfFadeOut
Fields.Description: The time, in seconds, at which the track's fade-out period starts. If the track has no fade-out, this should match the track's length.

Fields.Name: Loudness
Fields.Description: The overall loudness of a track in decibels (dB). Loudness values are averaged across the entire track and are useful for comparing relative loudness of tracks. Loudness is the quality of a sound that is the primary psychological correlate of physical strength (amplitude). Values typically range between -60 and 0 db.

Fields.Name: Tempo
Fields.Description: The overall estimated tempo of a track in beats per minute (BPM). In musical terminology, tempo is the speed or pace of a given piece and derives directly from the average beat duration.

Fields.Name: TempoConfidence
Fields.Description:
The confidence, from 0.0 to 1.0, of the reliability of the `tempo`.

**Constraints**: `>= 0`, `<= 1`

Fields.Name: TimeSignature
Fields.Description:
An estimated time signature. The time signature (meter) is a notational convention to specify how many beats are in each bar (or measure). The time signature ranges from 3 to 7 indicating time signatures of "3/4", to "7/4".

**Constraints**: `>= 3`, `<= 7`

Fields.Name: TimeSignatureConfidence
Fields.Description:
The confidence, from 0.0 to 1.0, of the reliability of the `time_signature`.

**Constraints**: `>= 0`, `<= 1`

Fields.Name: Key
Fields.Description:
The key the track is in. Integers map to pitches using standard [Pitch Class notation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pitch_class). E.g. 0 = C, 1 = C♯/D♭, 2 = D, and so on. If no key was detected, the value is -1.

**Constraints**: `>= -1`, `<= 11`

Fields.Name: KeyConfidence
Fields.Description:
The confidence, from 0.0 to 1.0, of the reliability of the `key`.

**Constraints**: `>= 0`, `<= 1`

Fields.Name: Mode
Fields.Description: Mode indicates the modality (major or minor) of a track, the type of scale from which its melodic content is derived. Major is represented by 1 and minor is 0.

Fields.Name: ModeConfidence
Fields.Description:
The confidence, from 0.0 to 1.0, of the reliability of the `mode`.

**Constraints**: `>= 0`, `<= 1`

Fields.Name: Codestring
Fields.Description: An [Echo Nest Musical Fingerprint (ENMFP)](https://academiccommons.columbia.edu/doi/10.7916/D8Q248M4) codestring for this track.

Fields.Name: CodeVersion
Fields.Description: A version number for the Echo Nest Musical Fingerprint format used in the codestring field.

Fields.Name: Echoprintstring
Fields.Description: An [EchoPrint](https://github.com/spotify/echoprint-codegen) codestring for this track.

Fields.Name: EchoprintVersion
Fields.Description: A version number for the EchoPrint format used in the echoprintstring field.

Fields.Name: Synchstring
Fields.Description: A [Synchstring](https://github.com/echonest/synchdata) for this track.

Fields.Name: SynchVersion
Fields.Description: A version number for the Synchstring used in the synchstring field.

Fields.Name: Rhythmstring
Fields.Description: A Rhythmstring for this track. The format of this string is similar to the Synchstring.

Fields.Name: RhythmVersion
Fields.Description: A version number for the Rhythmstring used in the rhythmstring field.

Nodes.Title: Track
Nodes.Name: Track
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Example.Text:
import com.spotify.api.ApiHelper;
import com.spotify.api.models.Track;
import java.io.IOException;

Track track = new Track.Builder()
    .numSamples(4585515)
    .duration(207.95985D)
    .sampleMd5("sample_md56")
    .offsetSeconds(0)
    .windowSeconds(0)
    .analysisSampleRate(22050)
    .analysisChannels(1)
    .endOfFadeIn(0D)
    .startOfFadeOut(201.13705D)
    .loudness(-5.883D)
    .tempo(118.211D)
    .tempoConfidence(0.73D)
    .timeSignature(4)
    .timeSignatureConfidence(0.994D)
    .key(9)
    .keyConfidence(0.408D)
    .mode(0)
    .modeConfidence(0.485D)
    .codeVersion(3.15D)
    .echoprintVersion(4.15D)
    .synchVersion(1D)
    .rhythmVersion(1D)
.additionalProperty("exampleAdditionalProperty", ApiHelper.deserialize("{\"key1\":\"val1\",\"key2\":\"val2\"}"))
    .build();

Fields.Name: Uri
Fields.Description: Spotify URI

Nodes.Title: Track 1
Nodes.Name: Track1
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type Object.*

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name for the new playlist, for example `"Your Coolest Playlist"`. This name does not need to be unique; a user may have several playlists with the same name.

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: Defaults to `true`. If `true` the playlist will be public, if `false` it will be private. To be able to create private playlists, the user must have granted the `playlist-modify-private` [scope](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes)

Fields.Name: Collaborative
Fields.Description: Defaults to `false`. If `true` the playlist will be collaborative. _**Note**: to create a collaborative playlist you must also set `public` to `false`. To create collaborative playlists you must have granted `playlist-modify-private` and `playlist-modify-public` [scopes](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes)._

Fields.Name: Description
Fields.Description: value for playlist description as displayed in Spotify Clients and in the Web API.

Nodes.Title: Include External
Nodes.Name: IncludeExternal
Nodes.Description:
If `include_external=audio` is specified it signals that the client can play externally hosted audio content, and marks
the content as playable in the response. By default externally hosted audio content is marked as unplayable in the response.

Example.Text:
import com.spotify.api.models.IncludeExternal;

IncludeExternal includeExternal = IncludeExternal.AUDIO;

import com.spotify.api.models.ItemType;

ItemType itemType = ItemType.ARTIST;

Nodes.Title: Mode
Nodes.Name: Mode
Nodes.Description: Indicates the modality (major or minor) of a section, the type of scale from which its melodic content is derived. This field will contain a 0 for "minor", a 1 for "major", or a -1 for no result. Note that the major key (e.g. C major) could more likely be confused with the minor key at 3 semitones lower (e.g. A minor) as both keys carry the same pitches.

Example.Text:
import com.spotify.api.models.Mode;

Mode mode = Mode.ENUM_MINUS1;

Nodes.Title: Reason
Nodes.Name: Reason
Nodes.Description:
The reason for the restriction. Albums may be restricted if the content is not available in a given market, to the user's subscription type, or when the user's account is set to not play explicit content.
Additional reasons may be added in the future.

Example.Text:
import com.spotify.api.models.Reason;

Reason reason = Reason.PRODUCT;

Nodes.Text: Class for holding logging configuration.
Methods

Data: Getter for the masking sensitive headers flag.
Converts this ApiLoggingConfiguration into string format.
Builds a new ApiLoggingConfiguration.Builder object. Creates the instance with the current state.

Nodes.Text: Class to build instances of ApiLoggingConfiguration.
Constructors

Data: Default Constructor to initiate builder with default properties.

Nodes.Text: Methods

Data: Set level for logging. Default level is INFO. Other available levels are `ERROR`, `WARN`, `DEBUG`, and `TRACE`.
Set Logger for logging. Here you can provide custom slf4j implementation if needed.
Set mask sensitive headers flag. Default value is true.
Builds a new [```ApiLoggingConfiguration```]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/ApiLoggingConfiguration) object using the set fields.

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Add Logger dependencies to Project
In order to provide custom logger, any implementation of the logging facade `org.slf4j.Logger` can be used by adding its dependencies to the `pom.xml`.

<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>log4j-api</artifactId>
        <version>${log4j.version}</version>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>log4j-core</artifactId>
        <version>${log4j.version}</version>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>log4j-slf4j2-impl</artifactId>
        <version>${log4j.version}</version>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>log4j-layout-template-json</artifactId>
        <version>${log4j.version}</version>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>
Create Logger Configuration

As we are using `org.apache.logging.log4j` dependency which itself provides its own logging API and implementation using the logging facade `org.slf4j.Logger`. So we would need to create a `log4j2.xml` configuration file and add it to the project's build path.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Configuration>
    <Appenders>
        <Console name="Console" target="SYSTEM_OUT">
            <JsonTemplateLayout eventTemplateUri="classpath:EcsLayout.json"/>
        </Console>
    </Appenders>
    <Loggers>
        <Root level="trace">
            <AppenderRef ref="Console" />
        </Root>
    </Loggers>
</Configuration>
Client Initialization with Custom Logger

The following example uses the `LoggerFactory.getLogger` to get the underlying log4j implementation from the project's build path for SpotifyWebApiWithFixesAndImprovementsFromSonalluxClient initialization.

Nodes.Text: This developer portal is powered by **APIMatic**. The same platform that generated this experience can turn any OpenAPI specification into a complete developer portal — interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing, and AI-assisted onboarding — in minutes.

Context Plugins load your full API context — docs, SDKs, code samples, and setup instructions — into AI coding environments like Claude Code and Cursor, so developers integrate your API through natural language instead of a wall of reference docs. A built-in portal copilot is available too.

API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides — including idiomatic, ready-to-run code samples complete with imports across all seven SDK languages. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Bring your API to APIMatic and ship a developer portal like this one in minutes.
See how APIMatic turns your API specification into a branded, AI-ready developer portal.
Dive into the Advanced Features Guide to set up and customize your own portal.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
Automatic **LLMs.txt generation** and per-page Markdown exports make your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.
